headline: Iran Signals Diplomatic Shift: Economic Gains for Nuclear Concessions
summary: On Sunday, Iran signaled it is willing to trade nuclear constraints for sanctions relief while trying to package any new U.S. agreement around fast-moving commercial upside for both countries, ahead of a second round of discussions between Tehran and Washington.

headline: Home Sellers Over 70 Accepting Less Amid Buyer's Market
summary: A new research from the Center for Retirement Research at Boston College pointed to a financial hit for homeowners who wait until their 70s or later to sell, with older sellers tending to accept lower prices than younger peers.

headline: Eu's Kallas Rejects Claims of Europe's 'Civilizational Erasure'
summary: On Sunday, European Union foreign policy chief Kaja Kallas used the Munich Security Conference to dismiss the idea that the continent is sliding toward "civilizational erasure," a claim she tied to a U.S. national security strategy released in December.

headline: Palmer Luckey Says Don't Trust A Boss Who Pretends Money Doesn't Matter, Oculus Founder Who Once Earned Minimum Wage Explains
summary: Palmer Luckey said employees should be wary of bosses who claim money doesn't matter, reflecting on his journey from minimum wage jobs to selling Oculus VR to Facebook for $2 billion and arguing that massive investment was essential for VR's survival.

headline: Charlie Munger Once Revealed A Simple Filter To Run All Of Life's Key Decisions: It Became His Most-Taught Investing Lesson
summary: Munger advises comparing every decision to the best alternative and choosing only if it's better, a filter used by Buffett and other investors

headline: Newsom Urges European Leaders To Look Beyond Trump: 'Trump Is Temporary'
summary: Gov. Gavin Newsom (D-Calif.) pressed European officials to keep building direct working relationships with U.S. states, arguing that President Trump will not define the U.S. forever.

headline: The $461B Boom That Advisors Can't Ignore
summary: Stock market up, tech and AI lead. Annuities shatter records for 4th year, driven by demand for guarantees in volatile market. Boomers and Gen X interested.

headline: European Governments Link Navalny's Death To Poisoning by Moscow
summary: On Saturday, five European governments said lab work on Alexei Navalny's remains detected epibatidine, a poison associated with South American dart frogs, and they argued the finding points to Russian responsibility for his 2024 death in an Arctic prison colony.

headline: Pelosi: Trump Is Trying To Disrupt Midterm Elections
summary: On Saturday, Nancy Pelosi said she plans to push back against what she described as President Donald Trump's efforts to shape election administration ahead of November's midterms, pointing to the House-passed SAVE Act and the administration's posture around voting oversight

headline: Weekend Round-Up: Bitcoin's Wild Ride, Schiff's Bitcoin Blast And Pudgy Penguins' Valentine's Day Pop-Up
summary: This week saw Bitcoin's volatile price fluctuations, Peter Schiff's criticism of Michael Saylor's Bitcoin plan, Gemini Space Station's stock rally, Ethereum's intersection with AI, and Pudgy Penguins' Valentine's Day NFT pop-up in NYC.

headline: Consumer Tech News (Feb 9-13): AI Energy Push, And Mixed Tech Earnings Dominate Headlines & More
summary: Amazon deploys 32 satellites, AI data centers face power scrutiny, and major tech earnings beat estimates as global markets digest space, energy, and semiconductor developments.

headline: Benzinga's 'Stock Whisper' Index: 5 Stocks Investors Secretly Monitor But Don't Talk About Yet
summary: The Benzinga Stock Whisper Index highlights five stocks seeing strong interest from readers during the week and a look at the catalysts driving the attention.

headline: Rivian, Magna International, And Akamai Are Among the Top 10 Large-Cap Gainers Last Week (Feb. 9-Feb. 13): Are the Others in Your Portfolio?
summary: Large-cap gainers were led by Magna, Rivian, and Generac after strong Q4 results and upbeat 2026 guidance, while QXO jumped on its Kodiak deal and Akamai/Vertiv rose on guidance plus analyst upgrades.

headline: Weekend Round-Up: Tesla's Semi Pricing, Trump's EPA Rollback, Pentagon's Chinese Military List And Waymo's Hyundai Deal
summary: This week featured major business and political developments, including Tesla's semi pricing undercutting competitors, the Trump administration's rollback of EPA regulations, the Pentagon's addition of Alibaba and BYD to a Chinese military list, Ford's $7 billion EV charge forecast, and Waymo's potential $2.5 billion deal with Hyundai.

headline: Pinterest, DraftKings, And Flutter Are Among Top 10 Large Cap Losers Last Week (Feb. 9-Feb. 13): Are the Others in Your Portfolio?
summary: Pinterest, DraftKings, and Astera Labs led last week's large-cap losers as cautious outlooks and post-earnings guidance/downgrades sparked a broad pullback across tech, housing, and gaming names.

headline: Spotify's Q4 Revenue Beat, Amazon's Super Bowl Ad Backlash, FTC's Letter To Apple And More: This Week In Tech
summary: This week saw a flurry of tech news, including Spotify's Q4 revenue beat, Amazon's controversial Super Bowl ad, the FTC's letter to Apple over political bias, Elon Musk's criticism of Anthropic, and Intel's penalty in India for discriminatory warranty practices.

headline: FTC Chairman Writes To Tim Cook, Siri Delay And More: This Week In Appleverse
summary: Apple navigated a week of highs and lows, from legal wins and market resilience in China to stock dips and FTC scrutiny.

headline: Tesla Expands AI Chip Design Efforts To South Korea, Actively Recruits Engineers
summary: Tesla actively recruits AI chip designers in South Korea, aligning with its vision to lead in chip production.

headline: Elon Musk Says Tesla Will Have 'Largest Fleet' Of Autonomous Vehicles As Far Into The Future As He Can Imagine
summary: Elon Musk predicts Tesla will have the biggest autonomous vehicle fleet in the future, aligning with their long-term goals and investments in AI.

headline: US Could Soon Enter A 'Debt Spiral', Exceeding Post-World War II Debt-To-GDP Record By 2030, Watchdog Says
summary: US debt is projected to surpass historical levels, reaching 120% of GDP by 2036. This spiral could pose a national security crisis, warns CBO.

headline: Elon Musk's xAI Faces Legal Action From NAACP Over Concerns Of Pollution From Its Memphis, Southaven Data Centers
summary: NAACP threatens to sue Elon Musk's xAI over alleged pollution caused by gas turbines near its data center, citing health issues and violation of Clean Air Act.

headline: Trump Tariffs Backfired, 130,000 Jobs Added In January And More: This Week In Economy
summary: Trump admits Fed chair mistake in 2017, US job revisions wipe out gains, China cuts Treasury buying, and Goldman spots memory chip winners.

headline: Warren Accuses Trump, Musk Of Undermining CFPB, Trump Tariffs Could Cost Households $1,300 And More: This Week In Politics
summary: Warren accuses Trump and Musk of undermining CFPB, tariffs could cost $1300/yr, Musk supports GOP, Trump censors student loan report.

headline: DOJ Sends Letter To Congress Listing Hundreds Of Names Mentioned In Epstein Files
summary: The DOJ submitted its report on Epstein materials to Congress, listing names from deceased celebrities to leading business executives.

headline: Two Full-Time Jobs Are Becoming The New Normal For Nearly Half A Million Americans
summary: The number of Americans holding multiple jobs is rising, with Gen Z increasingly relying on additional income streams to cover living costs.

headline: Warren Buffett Generated Double The Market's Returns—Until Regulation Changed The Game
summary: Chamath Palihapitiya said Warren Buffett's market-beating advantage vanished after the 2000 Reg FD rules ended selective disclosure.

headline: FDA Detects Toxic Patulin Levels In Tippy Toes Baby Food, Prompting Recall
summary: Initiative Foods is voluntarily recalling Tippy Toes Apple Pear Banana fruit puree due to toxic patulin contamination.

headline: Cathie Wood Says Tesla's Optimus Could Transform Factory, Home Life By 2028–2029
summary: Cathie Wood and ARK Invest see Tesla's Optimus robot as a transformative force for industry, homes, and the broader economy.

headline: Trump Fires Back At Bill Maher, Calls Dinner With Him 'A Total Waste Of Time'
summary: President Donald Trump criticized Bill Maher on Truth Social, calling their White House dinner a "total waste of time".
